libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Configure generated JVM tests with Test JVM args for OGNL reflective internals: --add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED and, if tests touch collection internals or mirror upstream's JDK17+ setup, --add-opens=java.base/java.util=ALL-UNNAMED. Do not add --illegal-access for JDK 25. Avoid setting ognl.security.manager globally; if a test exercises OGNL sandbox behavior, set and restore that system property within the test and account for SecurityManager installation being unsupported on recent JDKs.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7430 [Automation] java run fails for ognl:ognl:3.3.5; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: org.javassist:javassist:3.29.0-GA is already a transitive runtime dependency of ognl:ognl:3.3.5, so adding it explicitly would be redundant unless Gradle resolution proves otherwise.
Tests around OGNL's SecurityManager sandbox are fragile on JDK 25 because SecurityManager APIs are deprecated/disabled and may throw UnsupportedOperationException.
Tests that rely on Javassist dynamic class generation or classloader redefinition may need separate Native Image handling even if JVM tests pass.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OGNL does not need an extra dependency or service, but meaningful tests that exercise its reflective runtime internals need JVM module-opening test setup on modern JDKs.
